rss.title: Is it safe to swim at England's bathing sites?
rss.snippet: Signs warning people not to swim are in place at almost all of England's official inland river bathing sites due to concerns the water could be unsafe

rss.title: 'Don't swim' at 12 of 14 river bathing sites, as more locations announced
rss.snippet: Too much bacteria linked to faeces found at almost all England's designated river bathing sites

rss.title: White-tailed eagles to be released in Exmoor despite farmer warnings
rss.snippet: Some farmers fear the reintroduction of the UK's biggest bird of prey will threaten their livestock.

podcast:
[Return of the Flesh-Eaters] If a species is horrible enough, do we have the right to kill it forever?
Seventy years ago, a nightmare parasite feasted on the live flesh of warm-blooded creatures in North America: the screwworm. That is, until a young scientist named Edward F. Knipling discovered a crucial screwworm weakness and hatched a sweeping project to wipe them out. Knipling’s seemingly zany plan to spray screwworms out

youtube_items.title: [YT] Top 5 Climate Disasters of 2025 That Shook the World | Planet Pulse | N18G
youtube_items.summary: 2025 will be remembered as the year the climate crisis stopped knocking and kicked the door right down. Across continents, nature unleashed its fury. Record-breaking storms, catastrophic floods, raging wildfires, and deadly heat waves pushed communities to the edge. From South Asia's devastating monsoon floods that swallowed towns and displaced millions to powerful cyclones tearing through coastlines in India, Sri Lanka and the Caribbean. The human cost was staggering. Mountains collapsed under torrential rain. Forests burned through historic heat and cities struggled to cope with disasters arriving back to back. Scientists warned these were not frequents, but part of a dangerous new climate pattern driven by warming oceans, rising temperatures, and increasingly erratic weather systems. Ev

youtube_items.title: [YT] Inside the planet’s most urgent climate warning | Global Warning E1 | Featured Documentary
youtube_items.summary: Something momentous has happened. While we have been going about our daily lives, the world turned. Climate change is no longer a distant fear. It has now become a clear and current threat. Things that used to happen in geological time spans are now unraveling in our lifetimes. We are the pivot generation. Before us, normality, after us, potential chaos. It falls to us to apply the brakes that will prevent climate change becoming a runaway juggernaut that no one can stop. The future of human civilization will be what we make it. Global warming is a road map for what is happening to the planet and what we can do to stop it. summary: Климатът е в критично натоварено състояние, с резки увеличения на бедствията като урагани, наводнения и пожари. Студенти и научни институции работят по моделиране на климатичните промени, но реалните рискове остават високи.
key_developments: Великобритания предупреждава за незадължително плуване в 12 от 14 реки, поради високи нива на замърсение и нестабилност.
Виденията за 2025 г. показват резки климатични кризи с урагани, наводнения и пожари, които изправят хората на изпитание.
Проектите като ESCOMP/CESM и xgcm/xgcm са важни за анализиране на климатичните модели, но не заменят реалните действия.
relevance_to_goal: Повишава риска за устойчивост на цивилизацията, което противоречи на визията за достоен живот за всички.
